company_type: Technology - Enterprise Software and Cloud Services
segments: Oracle Fusion Sales, Service and Marketing

dependencies: Third-party manufacturing partners produce most hardware products
Reliance on sole sources for certain hardware components
Supply chain operations affected by industry consolidation and component constraints or shortages
Dependence on suppliers to develop, manufacture and deliver technologies and components for hardware products
Simplified supply chain processes by reducing number of third-party manufacturing partners and locations
Outsourcing of manufacturing, assembly, delivery and technology of hardware products to variety of companies, many located outside the U.S.
Reliance on third-party suppliers to provide equipment and components required to outfit data centers on timely basis
Industry supply capacity for AI accelerators, including graphics processing units, is competitive
risks: Supply chain shortages, including those arising from tariffs, changing trade policy or other macroeconomic developments

Natural disasters, political unrest, public health crises, changes to trade laws or regulations, tariffs and customs controls, port stoppages, shipping interruptions or other transportation disruptions affecting countries or regions where single source component vendors are located or where products are being shipped
Ongoing or future delays or inability to meet customer demand could cause loss of additional sales, delay revenue recognition or increase costs
Supply chain shortages have resulted in increases to costs of production of hardware products that may not be able to pass on to customers
Commitment to higher purchases and balances of hardware products relative to historical positions in response to shortages, increasing excess and obsolescence risk
Production problems or delays from third-party manufacturing partners or inability to meet demand for products on timely or cost-effective basis
Changes to trade laws or regulations, tariffs, sanctions and export or import controls affecting manufacturing
Delays in manufacturing could cause loss of additional sales, delayed revenue recognition or increase in hardware products expenses
Challenges arising from altering manufacturing strategies, suppliers, or locations
Rising costs for data center energy demands
Delays or inability to meet customer demand from third-party suppliers providing equipment and components for data centers
Less favorable terms with suppliers to avoid supply constraints for AI accelerators

regulatory_risks: Data privacy requirements and regulations (GDPR, U.S. federal and state privacy laws)
Healthcare industry regulations (HIPAA, electronic health data transmissions, processing of patient information, healthcare fraud, healthcare information sharing)
Medical device regulations in U.S. and other countries
CEHRT (Certified Electronic Health Record Technology) requirements and evolving certification and interoperability standards
Government procurement policy, priorities, regulations, technology initiatives and requirements changes
U.S. government cybersecurity requirements (FedRAMP authorization, DoD Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification)
Export control regulations and sanctions (U.S. and other jurisdictions)
Anti-bribery laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act - FCPA)
Competition/antitrust regulations
Tax laws and regulations (U.S. Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, OECD international tax system changes, 15% minimum tax, digital technology business taxes, withholding tax rules)
Labor relations laws
Foreign currency-related regulations
Market access regulations
Tariffs and import, export and general trade regulations

Environmental, social and governance (ESG) laws and regulations (environmental sustainability, supply chain labor practices, workforce health and safety, proper handling and disposal of chemicals and materials, local testing and labeling requirements)
Regulatory, market, carbon tax and competitive pressures regarding greenhouse gas emissions and energy mix for data center operations
AI laws and regulations (lack of comprehensive federal legislation, patchwork of existing and proposed frameworks in numerous jurisdictions)
Securities regulations for majority shareholder of publicly traded companies (Oracle Financial Services Software Limited in India, Oracle Corporation Japan)
Foreign direct investment, foreign subsidy, competition and antitrust laws affecting acquisitions
Income tax and non-income-based taxes (payroll, sales, use, property, value-added taxes)
Changes to U.S. federal tax law (potential extension of Tax Cuts and Jobs Act provisions, other legislative proposals)
OECD tax changes (15% minimum tax implementation in over 50 countries, proposed changes for greater taxing rights to market jurisdictions)
Country-specific tax law changes affecting international revenue streams, operations and cross-border transactions
Compliance with international and U.S. laws and regulations in international operations
Sanctions and embargos (U.S. law making it unlawful to provide internet hosting services to TikTok)
Economic and financial sanction laws and embargoes
Regulatory restrictions on serving certain customers or operating in specific jurisdictions
Government austerity measures in certain countries that may impair bank deposits or impose additional taxes
Local securities regulations for publicly traded subsidiaries
Works councils or similar bodies under applicable employment laws
Immigration and labor law policies affecting access to technical and professional talent

sanction_risks: Sanctions and export control regulations in U.S. and other jurisdictions requiring suspension or termination of business in or with certain countries and territories or with certain customers
Compliance with sanctions, export controls or other regulatory restrictions (and countermeasures) could prevent serving certain customers or restrict operations in specific jurisdictions
U.S. law making it unlawful to provide internet hosting services to TikTok (with current prohibition of enforcement by executive orders)
Potential loss of revenues and profits if prohibition on TikTok services is lifted or expires without resolution

Changes to sanctions or export control regulations requiring suspension or termination of business (including financial transactions) in or with certain countries and territories or with certain customers
